subject: english
learning_objectives: Identify common sources of spelling difficulty
Recognise examples of silent letters and homophones
Distinguish some British and American spellings

sections.heading: Areas of Focus
sections.content: The lesson covers words with silent letters, words with silent first or last letters, homophones, words of foreign origin, British and American spellings, and a list of commonly misspelt words.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
Silent-letter examples include scene, scissors, debt, doubt, handkerchief, solemn, psychology, knight, knee, gnash, write, lamb, bomb, tomb, chalet, and ballet.
Homophone pairs include maze/maize, mare/mayor, boy/buoy, flare/flair, pause/paws, and pail/pale.
British and American variants in the note include programme/program, colour/color, labelled/labeled, enrolment/enrollment, and manoeuvre/maneuver.
